mechanism_narrative: A lower or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and liquidity stress, which lifts equity multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates and policy restraint rise, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month moving average while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should hurt corporate revenue expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 60-day forward returns are not negative on average after 0.3pp UNRATE rises, or if the effect disappears in more than two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A broad retail sales acceleration in RSAFS signals stronger final demand and supports higher corporate revenue growth, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and above its 12-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in INDPRO together with stable PAYEMS indicates improving production and labor utilization, which boosts earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if the combined effect on 20-day returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing downturn that typically precedes weaker spending and risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drops are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails to persist across successive housing downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is slowing while RSAFS remains weak, the economy is transitioning into a demand recession that should depress equity earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth decelerates for two consecutive observations and RSAFS is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined slowdown signal does not produce below-baseline 20-day returns, or if positive returns dominate triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, modest payroll growth with unemployment near cycle lows supports consumption and earnings without forcing aggressive Fed tightening, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.5% and PAYEMS is positive over the last month
regime_hint: soft landing with contained policy pressure
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive when labor remains firm and policy is stable, or if the effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS is above 5.25%.
